search_session.purpose: tezkire mining: capture biographical entries, sample verses, manuscript citations, and codicological/paper claims from biographical-dictionary literature on Cihanşah Hakîkî (Jahānshāh Qaraqoyunlu, d. 872/1467). Results inform witness verification, sample-verse cross-checking, and the BL Or 9493 decorated-paper synthesis.

tezkires_consulted.title: TEIS Yesevi 'HAKÎKÎ, Cihânşâh' biographical entry
tezkires_consulted.author: Yesevi University Türk Edebiyatı İsimler Sözlüğü editorial team
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: Two complete ghazals (Tâ ki hüsnün... + Gelgil ki senden ayru...) and one complete tuyug (Dil-berün la'lini handân isterem...). Both ghazals close with Hakîkî takhalluṣ; both contain Hallâcî/Mansurian iconography ('Mansûr oldı der-meydân-ı aşk', 'Mansûrvâr egerçi meni dâra çekeler').
tezkires_consulted.codicological_claims: INDIRECT: TEIS quotes Uluç 2006 (pp. 28, 66) on Pīr Budāq's patronage of nakış (illumination) and cilt (binding): establishes Turkmen-period book-arts patronage chain. NO direct claims about Cihanşah's Divan manuscripts.
tezkires_consulted.biographical_evaluation_summary: Cihanşah portrayed as legitimate ruler-poet contributor to Azerbaijani Turkic + Persian literature; Hurufi-influenced; bilingual; in continuum with Nesîmî and Mansurian mysticism. Patron of arts including book arts via son Pīr Budāq.

tezkires_consulted.title: İslâm Ansiklopedisi (MEB) C. III, 'Cihan-Şah'
tezkires_consulted.author: Mükrimin H. Yınanç
tezkires_consulted.edition_used: MEB 1945, İstanbul, pp. 173-189 (= PDF pp. 89-96 of Cilt III)
tezkires_consulted.access_method: Direct HTTP fetch download → pypdf text extraction
manuscripts_cited: BnF Bibl. Nat. Suppl. Pers. 161-171, 208-209 (Cihanşah/Pir Budak letters to Mamluk sultans)
BnF Suppl. Pers. 1815 (Sultan Abu Sa'id letters to Cihanşah)
BnF Suppl. Pers. 190 + 301 (Kadı Nūr-Allah Mar'aşi, Majālis al-mu'minīn)
BnF Suppl. Pers. 1330 (Haydar Râzi, Macma' al-tavârih)
Esad Efendi 2125 ('Abd al-Razzāq Samarqandī, Maṭlaʿ al-saʿdayn)
Esad Efendi 3340 (Cihanşah's vakfiye for Tabriz hayrat: VERIFIED, also in TDV Konukçu 1993)
Esad Efendi 3369 (Uzun Hasan letter to Fatih about Mardin victory)
Esad Efendi 3673 (Husâmeddin-zâde mecmuası, var. 59ff.: Cihanşah long reply)
Devletşâh, Tazkirat al-shu'arā: Browne Leyden 1901 ed., p. 554
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: Yınanç quotes ZERO sample verses from Hakîkî's Divan. Treatment is purely political-historical and bibliographic.
tezkires_consulted.codicological_claims: ZERO claims about Cihanşah's Divan manuscripts. Single relevant codicological lead: Esad Efendi 3340 = Cihanşah's vakfiye for Tabriz hayrat (charitable foundations): NOT a Divan witness, but the most-likely-decorated manuscript object connected to Cihanşah's name in Turkish collections (vakfiyes were often calligraphically/decoratively elaborate). unresolved question for authenticated YEK portal future research.
tezkires_consulted.biographical_evaluation_summary: Cihanşah characterised as: (1) bigger than other Qara-Qoyunlu rulers; (2) considered himself successor to Ilkhanid sovereigns; (3) wrote Persian + Turkish poems under Hakîkî pen-name 'in this century when Arab poetry had collapsed and Persian poetry was in decay'; (4) patron of scholars at Tabriz medrese; (5) Mar'aşi labels him Shi'i: Yınanç rebuts this attribution. Religious-philosophical attitudes 'partial truth' to charges of irreligion, 'more moderate than his brothers'.

tezkires_consulted.title: TDV İslâm Ansiklopedisi C. 7, 'Cihan Şah'
tezkires_consulted.author: Enver Konukçu
tezkires_consulted.edition_used: TDV İslam Ansiklopedisi C. 7, İstanbul 1993, p. 536
tezkires_consulted.access_method: direct HTTP fetch (returned summary; full text not accessible)
manuscripts_cited: Süleymaniye Esad Efendi 3340 (Cihanşah's vakfiye for Tabriz charitable foundations)
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: ZERO sample verses. The entry preserves Minorsky 1954's text statistics (105 Persian / 87 Turkish ghazals / 32 rubaʿis): pre-Macit numbers.
tezkires_consulted.codicological_claims: ZERO direct claims. Only mentions Esad Efendi 3340 as a vakfiye witness.
tezkires_consulted.biographical_evaluation_summary: Strictly biographical. Notes Hakîkî pen-name + bilingual poetry + Minorsky as the source for verse examples.

tezkires_consulted.title: Câmî Münşeât poem in praise of Cihanşah's Divan
tezkires_consulted.author: ʿAbd al-Raḥmān Jāmī (d. 898/1492)
tezkires_consulted.edition_used: Hikmet 1991 Turkish translation (Çev. M. N. Gencosman) of Hikmet's *Mollâ Câmî*; reproduced verbatim in Macit 2000 Bilig 13 pp. 10-11
tezkires_consulted.access_method: via Macit 2000 Bilig 13 PDF (376 KB) downloaded by direct HTTP fetch
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: Reproduces Câmî's full verse-response (28 couplets in Turkish trans.) but quotes NO sample of Cihanşah's own poetry. The response is a Persian poem about Cihanşah's Divan, not a quotation from it.
tezkires_consulted.codicological_claims: TENTATIVE: Câmî describes the Divan-manuscript as 'içi mücevher hokkasını andıran gerçek incilerle dolu mübarek bir kitap' (a blessed book whose interior resembles a casket of jewels filled with genuine pearls). PANEGYRIC METAPHOR: NOT decoration evidence. Methodologically unsafe to read as decorated-paper claim. However, confirms high-quality manuscript copies of the Divan circulated as gifts at the Timurid-Aq Qoyunlu intellectual elite level.
tezkires_consulted.biographical_evaluation_summary: Câmî praises Cihanşah's Divan as containing 'gazeller, mesneviler, sûret ve manâya ait sırlar' (ghazals, mathnawīs, secrets of form and meaning): supports a masnavī-bearing form for Cihanşah's lifetime divan; specific codex/count assignment to Mat 965 / Süleymaniye Fatih 3808 remains unresolved pending direct collation.

tezkires_consulted.title: Macit, 'Cihânşâh ve Türkçe Şiirleri': Bilig 13 (2000)
tezkires_consulted.author: Doç. Dr. Muhsin Macit
tezkires_consulted.edition_used: Bilig 13 (Bahar 2000), pp. 9-17; English abstract pp. 18-19, open-access PDF
tezkires_consulted.access_method: direct HTTP fetch + pypdf extraction (376 KB PDF)
manuscripts_cited: British Museum Or. 9493 (London): 113 P + 92 T + 33 tuyughs per Macit revision
Süleymaniye Fatih 3808: 109 P + 32 mesnevis + 94 T + 28 tuyughs
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: 13 ghazals + 3 tuyughs reproduced as matlaʿ (opening couplets only); all overlap with Nesîmî attributions in Araslı 1973 / Ayan 1990 / Kahramanof 1973 editions of Nesîmî Divan. CRITICAL TEXTUAL LIFELINES for cross-checking witnesses since manuscript folio refs (S=Süleymaniye Fatih 3808, L=BL Or 9493) are explicit.
tezkires_consulted.codicological_claims: ZERO. Macit gives no paper, decoration, illumination, or binding details for ANY of the 4 witnesses.
tezkires_consulted.biographical_evaluation_summary: Cihanşah is bilingual ruler-poet; Hurufi-influenced but suppressed Hurufi rebellion 1441; Hallâcî-Mansurian mystical imagery; closer textually to Nesîmî than Minorsky 1954 acknowledged. Câmî's Münşeât response supports a masnavī-bearing form within Cihanşah's lifetime; specific codex assignment to Mat 965 / Süleymaniye remains unresolved pending direct collation.

tezkires_consulted.title: Devletşâh, Tezkiretü'ş-Şuʿarâ (1487): Lugal Vol 1-3
tezkires_consulted.author: Devletşâh-ı Semerkandî
tezkires_consulted.edition_used: Necati Lugal trans. İstanbul 1977 Tercüman 1001 Temel Eser: Vols 1-3 retrieved via silo.tips full-text mirror; Vol 4 (containing dedicated Cihanşah entry per TEIS Yesevi citation 'Devletşâh 1977: 537') NOT retrieved
tezkires_consulted.access_method: third-party HTML mirroring + direct HTTP fetch
tezkires_consulted.haqiqi_entry_present: partial: political mentions only in Vols 1-3; dedicated entry in Vol 4 not retrieved
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: ZERO from the dedicated entry (not retrieved). 5 political-narrative mentions of Cihanşah Türkmen / Cihanşah b. Kara Yusuf in Şahruh-era / Aq Qoyunlu rivalry contexts. Vol 1-3 ends with 'Cihanşah ve evlâdının hal tercümesi bundan sonra gelecektir' (forward reference).
tezkires_consulted.codicological_claims: ZERO. Tezkires of this genre never engage codicology.
tezkires_consulted.biographical_evaluation_summary: Per TEIS quotation of Lugal Vol 4 p. 537: 'Cihanşah yetmiş yıl ömür sürdü' (lived 70 years): used by Kurbanov 1990 to compute birth year 1399. Devletşâh's tezkire (1487, 20 years post-Cihanşah) is the earliest external acknowledgement of Cihanşah-as-poet outside Cihanşah's own circle.

tezkires_consulted.title: Encyclopaedia Iranica 'JAHĀNŠĀH QARĀ QOYUNLU' (cross-reference stub)
tezkires_consulted.author: n/a (cross-reference only)
tezkires_consulted.access_method: direct HTTP fetch (HTTP 403); third-party HTML mirroring returned page metadata only: no article body
tezkires_consulted.sample_verses_summary: ZERO. Stub redirects to Qara Qoyunlu main entry which is itself 404 at all tested slugs (qara-qoyunlu, qara-qoyunlu-confederation, kara-koyunlu, qaraqoyunlu, haqiqi-2, haqiqi-jahanshah). The full Iranica Jahanshah entry is presumably in printed Vol XIV (J) but not migrated online.

supplementary_data_for_existing_witnesse.data: Câmî's Münşeât poem indicates Cihanşah's lifetime Divan contained 'gazeller, mesneviler, sûret ve manâya ait sırlar', so the mesnevi component is authorial. BL Or 9493 (no mesnevis) preserves a narrower form; specific assignment of the masnavī-bearing form to the Mat 965 / Süleymaniye Fatih 3808 codices remains unresolved pending direct collation.

13 ghazals + 3 tuyughs in BL Or 9493 (folios listed in Macit 2000 p. 13) overlap textually with Nesîmî Divan editions. These constitute textual lifelines for cross-checking Hakîkî-attributed material against later Nesîmî manuscript collections (the mecmûʿa sweep Nesîmî-adjacency heuristic confirmed empirically).

Mat 965 (1474-75) PRE-DATES every known Nesîmī manuscript witness. When ghazals are attributed to both Hakîkî AND Nesîmî in different printed editions, the chronology of Mat 965's existence supports Hakîkî priority of attribution.

Süleymaniye Esad Efendi 3340 = Cihanşah's vakfiye for Tabriz charitable foundations. NOT a Divan witness, but the most-likely-decorated manuscript object directly connected to Cihanşah's name in Turkish collections (vakfiyes are often calligraphically/decoratively elaborate). unresolved question for authenticated YEK portal future research visualization.

homonymous_hakikis_distinguished: HAKÎKÎ Cihânşâh (d. 1467): TARGET, Qaraqoyunlu ruler, bilingual Persian/Turkish poet
HAKÎKÎ Mustafa Bağdâdî: different poet, often confused; TEIS Yesevi has separate entry
HAKÎKÎZÂDE Osman Efendi (d. 1627): Ottoman dervish poet
rejected_attributions: Verses ostensibly attributed to Hakîkî in late mecmuʿas without Nesîmî disambiguation should be cross-checked against the 13 ghazals + 3 tuyughs that Macit 2000 establishes as appearing in BOTH Nesîmî and Hakîkî manuscript traditions. Default to Hakîkî only if (a) the verse is in Mat 965 (1474-75) or BL Or 9493 (1488), (b) matches the Hakîkî-takhalluṣ usage pattern, or (c) is in a clearly Hakîkî-titled witness.

codicological_findings_for_bl_or_9493_de.summary: Tezkire literature provides ZERO direct codicological / paper / decoration evidence for any of the TEIS Yesevi 5 Cihanşah Hakîkî witnesses. This is a structural feature of the tezkire genre, not a gap that further tezkire mining can fill. The most relevant codicological lead surfaced by this scope is INDIRECT: TEIS Yesevi (citing Uluç 2006 pp. 28, 66) confirms that Pīr Budāq, Cihanşah's son, patronised illumination (nakış) and binding (cilt) at Cihanşah's court: establishing the Turkmen-period book-arts patronage chain in which BL Or 9493 (1488 Isfahan, 21 years post-Cihanşah) was produced. Câmî's panegyric metaphor 'içi mücevher hokkasını andıran gerçek incilerle dolu mübarek bir kitap' is methodologically unsafe to read as decoration evidence: it is metaphor about textual content, not material decoration.

summary_metrics.total_sample_verses_captured_verbatim: 3 full ghazals + 1 full tuyug + 16 ghazal/tuyug matlaʿ pairs
